_meta.description: Translation table: Rocksmith gear knob → VST parameter. Curated around Kilohearts Essentials (free) — consistent param names across the entire bundle.

<rs_knob_name>.param: <exact VST param name as shown in the in-app editor>
<rs_knob_name>.scale: <float, multiply RS value by this. 0.01 for 0-100 → 0-1; 0.0005 for ms 0-2000; 1.0 for direct dB / semitones>
<rs_knob_name>.invert: <optional bool, replaces v with (1-v) after scale>

kilohearts_param_naming_convention._note: KHS modules use consistent param names across the entire Essentials bundle. Common params you'll see in every module:
kilohearts_param_naming_convention.mix: Dry/Wet (0-1) — some older docs show 'Mix'; the in-app editor will show the real name

notes: If a param name doesn't match, open the in-app VST editor for that gear, copy the exact label from a slider, and paste here. The lookup is case-insensitive but otherwise exact.
VST name stems below are lowercased to match the case-insensitive lookup. Plugin files on disk are 'kHs Chorus.vst3' etc. — the matcher strips '.vst3' and lowercases automatically.

EQ pedals (EQ5/EQ8/BassEQ8): knobs are absolute frequencies (e.g. '5700': 5 = +5 dB at 5700 Hz). Verified band layouts (from inspect_rs_knobs.py against a real library): EQ5 = 63/250/750/2200/5700 Hz; EQ8 = 50/100/200/400/800/1600/3200/6400 Hz; BassEQ8 = 30/75/185/460/1100/2700/6800/16000 Hz. kHs 3-Band EQ absorbs three of those — the entries below pick the most musical bands per pedal. The other RS frequencies are silently dropped (lossy). For lossless mapping, switch the gear to Voxengo Marvel GEQ (16-band) and add a per-pedal entry under 'voxengo marvel geq'.

Rack_StudioEQ (4-band parametric: Bass/LoMid/HiMid/Treble with Q + Freq knobs) is the highest-coverage EQ in the library. Currently lossy-mapped to kHs 3-Band (gain bands only — Q/Freq dropped). For full fidelity switch the gear to TDR Nova (4-band parametric, free) and add a 'tdr nova' entry mapping <Band>+<BandFreq>+<BandQ> per band.

Melda free (MFreeFXBundle): MTremolo, MVibrato, MFlanger, MPhaser, MRingModulator, MEqualizer, MCompressor are now PRIMARY for those categories alongside the existing KHS entries. Melda paramId convention is consistent across the family — verifying one editor (e.g. MTremolo) validates all 7 Melda mappings.

_static block: a VST entry may include `"_static": {"<vst_param_name>": <normalized_value>, ...}` to apply UNCONDITIONAL params regardless of RS knobs. Used to pin curated character knobs (e.g. kHs Distortion Type + Dynamics) so every fuzz pedal sounds like a fuzz, every overdrive sounds like an overdrive. Values are normalized [0,1]. For stepped/enum params, verify the dropdown order via `await window.rbSweepParam(slotId, paramName)` from DevTools — names + numeric ranges differ across plugins.

kHs Distortion subtype convention (user-curated, verified via rbSweepParam): the `Type` param has 6 discrete modes — Overdrive (0.0), Saturate (0.2), Foldback (0.4), Sine (0.6), Hard Clip (0.8), Quantize (1.0). Fuzz pedals → Type=0.2 (Saturate); overdrive pedals → Type=0.0 (Overdrive); distortion pedals → Type=0.8 (Hard Clip). All three pin Dynamics=1.0 so the pedal's response tracks the player's pick attack.

Mid._hint: The AC30 Top Boost reference has no Mid pot; this fills or cuts the post-tone-stack body while preserving Rocksmith's Mid knob.

Pres._hint: Pres maps to the inverse of the AC30 cut/presence behavior: higher Rocksmith Pres means less cut and more EL84/speaker bite.

Bright._hint: Rocksmith stores Bright as 0/1; EN30 treats values >= 0.5 as the brilliant/top-boost voicing.

Gain._hint: Rocksmith's single Gain blends the Super-Sonic 22 from Vintage/Fat clean behavior into the Burn channel's cascaded gain stages.
5E3 volume behavior: Rocksmith Gain drives the 12AY7 preamp and cathode-biased 6V6 output section.

Bass._hint: The real 57 Deluxe has no Bass pot; this controls low-end coupling and tweed thump while tightening high-gain flub.

Mid._hint: The real 5E3 has no Mid pot; this controls the tweed mid body instead of a blackface-style scoop.

Treble._hint: Maps to the single 5E3 Tone circuit.

Pres._hint: The schematic has no presence pot; this adds/removes power-amp and speaker bite.

Gain._hint: Rocksmith Gain drives the 5F6-A preamp and 5881-style output section.

Bass._hint: Maps to the Bassman FMV tone stack bass control.

Mid._hint: Maps to the Bassman FMV tone stack middle control.

Treble._hint: Maps to the Bassman FMV tone stack treble control and bright-channel blend.

Pres._hint: Maps to the Bassman presence circuit and upper speaker bite.

Gain._hint: Rocksmith Gain morphs the DSL100 from Classic Clean into Classic Crunch and Ultra OD1.

Bass._hint: Maps to the DSL tone stack bass control plus low-end tightening at high gain.

Mid._hint: Maps to the DSL tone stack middle control; low Mid at high Gain increases the Tone Shift-style scoop.

Treble._hint: Maps to the DSL tone stack treble control and high-gain bite.

Pres._hint: Maps to the upper feedback-loop presence and speaker bite.

Res._hint: Maps to the DSL deep/resonance low-feedback behavior.

Gain._hint: Rocksmith Gain morphs the Dual Rectifier Red channel through the local Red G2/G5/G8 curation.

Bass._hint: Maps to the Dual Rectifier Red tone stack bass control with high-gain low-end tightening.

Mid._hint: Maps to the Red tone stack mid control; high Gain adds the Rectifier modern scoop around it.

Treble._hint: Maps to the Red tone stack treble control and cascaded-stage bite.

Pres._hint: Maps to the presence and feedback network in the power amp.

MidShift._hint: RS MidShift is 275-3000 Hz. AcousticSimulator stores it normalized: (Hz - 275) / 2725.

Rate._hint: Rocksmith base Chorus Rate is stored around 0..6; normalize that to the CE-2 style LFO range.
RS chorus Rate maps to kHs Chorus Rate slider POSITION directly: RS=4 → slider at 4%. Plugin's internal Rate-display conversion (log 0.05..10 Hz) does the rest. User-verified intent.
Rocksmith Chorus 2 Rate is stored as 0..10, so the bundled plugin receives Rate/10 and applies its BBD speed curve internally.
RS chorus Rate maps to kHs Chorus Rate slider POSITION directly: RS=4 → slider at 4%. Plugin's internal Rate-display conversion (log 0.05..10 Hz) does the rest. User-verified intent.
Rocksmith Digital Chorus Rate is stored as a small 0..10-ish value, so the bundled plugin receives Rate/10 and applies its digital LFO curve internally.

LoFilter._hint: Higher RS LoFilter moves the digital chorus high-pass cutoff up, matching the schematic's low-filter cut control.

HiFilter._hint: Higher RS HiFilter lowers the wet-path low-pass cutoff, matching the schematic's high-filter cut control.

Rate._hint: RS chorus Rate maps to kHs Chorus Rate slider POSITION directly: RS=4 → slider at 4%. Plugin's internal Rate-display conversion (log 0.05..10 Hz) does the rest. User-verified intent.
Rocksmith Vintage/Analog Chorus Rate is stored around 1..2, so the bundled plugin receives Rate/10 and keeps the LFO in a slow analog range.
RS chorus Rate maps to kHs Chorus Rate slider POSITION directly: RS=4 → slider at 4%. Plugin's internal Rate-display conversion (log 0.05..10 Hz) does the rest. User-verified intent.
RS Rate → LFO speed 0..1 (0.1..8 Hz).

LoFilter._hint: RS LoFilter → wet high-pass 0..1 (keeps lows dry).

Mix._hint: RS Mix → dry/wet 0..1.

Rate._hint: RS chorus Rate maps to kHs Chorus Rate slider POSITION directly: RS=4 → slider at 4%. Plugin's internal Rate-display conversion (log 0.05..10 Hz) does the rest. User-verified intent.

Clones._hint: Rocksmith uses low Clones values like 1/11/35. Treat 1 as 'one audible clone' rather than 1%; the plugin's internal curve then turns larger values into more/spread-out voices.

Regen._hint: Rocksmith calls the feedback control Regen; the bundled MF-108M style flanger uses it as BBD feedback/regeneration.

Feedback._hint: Alias for any extracted rows that use Feedback instead of Regen.

Rate._hint: RS Rate→LFO speed 0..1 (0.05..5 Hz).

Filter._hint: RS Filter→Resonance/feedback 0..1 (the jet).

Mix._hint: RS Mix→dry/wet 0..1.

0._hint: Alias for extracted rows that carried the single Phaser 363 knob as index 0.

Mix._hint: Rocksmith Tremolo Mix is the depth/intensity control for the bundled Colorsound-style tremolo.
RS 'Mix' on a tremolo is effectively depth

Speed._hint: RS Speed 0-100 → 1-10 Hz (display Hz; range log[0.01,20] in apply_vst_state)

Mix._hint: RS Mix on a tremolo = depth (MTremolo has no Dry/Wet param)

Depth._hint: Rocksmith Amp Trem Depth maps directly to the optical gain-reduction depth.

Speed._hint: RS Speed 0-100 → 1-10 Hz (display Hz; range log[0.01,20] in apply_vst_state)

Mix._hint: RS Mix on a tremolo = depth (MTremolo has no Dry/Wet param)
Rocksmith Multi Trem Mix is tremolo depth.

Speed._hint: RS Speed 0-100 → 1-10 Hz (display Hz; range log[0.01,20] in apply_vst_state)

Mix._hint: RS Mix on a tremolo = depth (MTremolo has no Dry/Wet param)

Time._hint: RS Time controls the spring tank decay.

Depth._hint: RS Depth controls dwell, transient drip, and resonant spring intensity.

Tone._hint: RS Tone (brightness) → less Dampen. Inverted.

Time._hint: Rocksmith Time controls the digital reverb decay.

Depth._hint: Rocksmith Depth controls digital room depth, pre-delay and tail density.

Tone._hint: Rocksmith Tone controls brightness/damping.
RS Tone (brightness) → less Dampen. Inverted.
RS Tone (brightness) → less Dampen. Inverted.

Depth._hint: Rocksmith Tube Spring Depth controls dwell, tank decay, and spring drip intensity.

Time._hint: RS Time is milliseconds. AnalogDelay stores it as ms / 2000 and reconstructs the actual delay time internally.
RS Time is milliseconds. ValveEcho stores it as ms / 2000 and reconstructs a Binson-style multi-head delay internally.
RS Time is milliseconds. NpnDelay stores it as ms / 2000 and clamps internally to a DM-2 style 20-420 ms range so Rocksmith presets around 396 ms survive.
RS Time is milliseconds. NoFiEcho stores it as ms / 2000 and reconstructs the actual echo time internally.
RS Time is observed 20-900 ms. ModDelay stores it normalized: (ms - 20) / 880.

Rate._hint: RS Rate is observed 0-3.5 Hz. ModDelay stores it normalized: Hz / 3.5.

Tone._hint: RS Tone 0-100 → kHs Distortion Bias 0-1. Bias=0.5 (RS Tone 50) is symmetric clipping; <0.5 darker, >0.5 brighter character via asymmetric harmonics.

Filter._hint: RS Filter 0-100 → kHs Dynamics 0-1. Overrides the _static Dynamics=1.0 pin when this gear exposes a Filter knob.

Blend._hint: RS Blend 0-100 → kHs Dry/Wet 0-1. Most distortion pedals don't expose Blend; Bass_Pedal_BassOverdrive does.

Voice._hint: Rocksmith Voice is a binary 0/1 switch. CustomDrive treats <0.5 as the smoother voice and >=0.5 as the tighter/brighter voice.

Tone._hint: RS Tone 0-100 → kHs Distortion Bias 0-1. Bias=0.5 (RS Tone 50) is symmetric clipping; <0.5 darker, >0.5 brighter character via asymmetric harmonics.

Filter._hint: RS Filter 0-100 → kHs Dynamics 0-1. Overrides the _static Dynamics=1.0 pin when this gear exposes a Filter knob.

Blend._hint: RS Blend 0-100 → kHs Dry/Wet 0-1. Most distortion pedals don't expose Blend; Bass_Pedal_BassOverdrive does.

Tone._hint: RS Tone 0-100 -> LineDrive Tone 0-1. Lower values are darker and thicker; higher values open the OS-2 style post-clipping tone network.

Middle._hint: Alias for Rocksmith exports that spell the GV-2 middle control as Middle.

Filter._hint: RS Filter 0-100 → B3K Grunt 0..1 (low end into the clipper).

Tone._hint: RS Tone 0-100 → B3K Attack 0..1 (treble of the distortion).
RS Tone 0-100 → kHs Distortion Bias 0-1. Bias=0.5 (RS Tone 50) is symmetric clipping; <0.5 darker, >0.5 brighter character via asymmetric harmonics.

Filter._hint: RS Filter 0-100 → kHs Dynamics 0-1. Overrides the _static Dynamics=1.0 pin when this gear exposes a Filter knob.

Blend._hint: RS Blend 0-100 → kHs Dry/Wet 0-1. Most distortion pedals don't expose Blend; Bass_Pedal_BassOverdrive does.

Gain._hint: Halve scale — booster shouldn't fully saturate

Tone._hint: RS Tone 0-100 → kHs Distortion Bias 0-1. Bias=0.5 (RS Tone 50) is symmetric clipping; <0.5 darker, >0.5 brighter character via asymmetric harmonics.

Filter._hint: RS Filter 0-100 → kHs Dynamics 0-1. Overrides the _static Dynamics=1.0 pin when this gear exposes a Filter knob.

Blend._hint: RS Blend 0-100 → kHs Dry/Wet 0-1. Most distortion pedals don't expose Blend; Bass_Pedal_BassOverdrive does.

Tone._hint: RS Tone 0-100 -> SuperDrive Tone 0-1. Lower values keep the SD-1 darker and mid-forward; higher values open the top end after clipping.
RS Tone 0-100 -> StandardDistortion Tone 0-1. Lower values are darker/thicker; higher values open the DS-1 style cutting top end.
RS Tone 0-100 -> VintageDistortion Tone 0-1. Lower values are darker and thicker; higher values open the DOD 250 style top end.
RS Tone 0-100 -> AlloyDistortion Tone 0-1. Lower values emphasize the HM-2 low color; higher values add chainsaw high color.

Rate._hint: RS Rate 0-100 → LFO wobble speed 0..1.

Mix._hint: RS Mix 0-100 → wet/dry 0..1.

Filter._hint: RS Filter 0-100 → base centre frequency 0..1.

Mix._hint: RS Mix 0-100 → wet/dry 0..1.

Filter._hint: RS Filter 0-100 → repeat tone 0..1 (dark↔bright).

Mix._hint: RS Mix 0-100 → wet/dry 0..1.

Filter._hint: RS Filter 0-100 → repeat tone 0..1 (dark↔bright).

Mix._hint: RS Mix 0-100 → dry/sub-octave blend 0..1.

Tone._hint: RS Tone 0-100 → sub-octave low-pass 0..1 (dark↔open).

Rate._hint: RS Rate 0-100 → LFO speed 0..1.

Mix._hint: RS Mix 0-100 → dry/wet notch blend 0..1.

Filter._hint: RS Filter 0-100 → sweep centre frequency 0..1.

Bass._hint: RS Bass ±15 dB → low-shelf gain 0..1 (0.5 = flat).

Mid._hint: RS Mid ±15 dB → mid-peak gain 0..1 (0.5 = flat).

Treble._hint: RS Treble ±15 dB → high-shelf gain 0..1 (0.5 = flat).

LoShift._hint: RS LoShift switch 0/1 → Bass Boost toggle (drops low corner + adds bottom).

MidShift._hint: RS MidShift switch 0/1 → Mid Shift toggle (600 Hz → 1.2 kHz).

Tone._hint: RS Tone 0-100 → pre-clip brightness 0..1 (grit character).

Filter._hint: RS Filter 0-100 → RAT Filter low-pass 0..1 (dark↔bright).

Tone._hint: RS Tone 0-100 → kHs Distortion Bias 0-1. Bias=0.5 (RS Tone 50) is symmetric clipping; <0.5 darker, >0.5 brighter character via asymmetric harmonics.

Filter._hint: RS Filter 0-100 → kHs Dynamics 0-1. Overrides the _static Dynamics=1.0 pin when this gear exposes a Filter knob.

Blend._hint: RS Blend 0-100 → kHs Dry/Wet 0-1. Most distortion pedals don't expose Blend; Bass_Pedal_BassOverdrive does.

Tone._hint: RS Tone 0-100 → kHs Distortion Bias 0-1. Bias=0.5 (RS Tone 50) is symmetric clipping; <0.5 darker, >0.5 brighter character via asymmetric harmonics.

Blend._hint: RS Blend 0-100 → kHs Dry/Wet 0-1. Most distortion pedals don't expose Blend; Bass_Pedal_BassOverdrive does.

Tone._hint: RS Tone 0-100 → kHs Distortion Bias 0-1. Bias=0.5 (RS Tone 50) is symmetric clipping; <0.5 darker, >0.5 brighter character via asymmetric harmonics.

Filter._hint: RS Filter 0-100 → kHs Dynamics 0-1. Overrides the _static Dynamics=1.0 pin when this gear exposes a Filter knob.

Blend._hint: RS Blend 0-100 → kHs Dry/Wet 0-1. Most distortion pedals don't expose Blend; Bass_Pedal_BassOverdrive does.

Tone._hint: Continuous Super-Fuzz tone-switch balance: lower is thicker/nasal, higher is brighter and more scooped.
RS Tone 0-100 → kHs Distortion Bias 0-1. Bias=0.5 (RS Tone 50) is symmetric clipping; <0.5 darker, >0.5 brighter character via asymmetric harmonics.

Filter._hint: RS Filter 0-100 → kHs Dynamics 0-1. Overrides the _static Dynamics=1.0 pin when this gear exposes a Filter knob.

Blend._hint: RS Blend 0-100 → kHs Dry/Wet 0-1. Most distortion pedals don't expose Blend; Bass_Pedal_BassOverdrive does.

Tone._hint: Continuous Big Muff V1 tone stack balance: lower is darker/thicker, higher is brighter and more scooped.
RS Tone 0-100 → kHs Distortion Bias 0-1. Bias=0.5 (RS Tone 50) is symmetric clipping; <0.5 darker, >0.5 brighter character via asymmetric harmonics.

Filter._hint: RS Filter 0-100 → kHs Dynamics 0-1. Overrides the _static Dynamics=1.0 pin when this gear exposes a Filter knob.

Blend._hint: RS Blend 0-100 → kHs Dry/Wet 0-1. Most distortion pedals don't expose Blend; Bass_Pedal_BassOverdrive does.

Tone._hint: RS Tone 0-100 → Big Muff tone stack 0..1 (LP↔HP, mid scoop at 0.5).

Filter._hint: RS Filter 0-100 → clean low-end blend 0..1 (the bass-specific dry/bass mix).

Tone._hint: RS Tone 0-100 → kHs Distortion Bias 0-1. Bias=0.5 (RS Tone 50) is symmetric clipping; <0.5 darker, >0.5 brighter character via asymmetric harmonics.

Filter._hint: RS Filter 0-100 → kHs Dynamics 0-1. Overrides the _static Dynamics=1.0 pin when this gear exposes a Filter knob.

Blend._hint: RS Blend 0-100 → kHs Dry/Wet 0-1. Most distortion pedals don't expose Blend; Bass_Pedal_BassOverdrive does.

Release._hint: RS Release reaches ~540 ms in the DB. Normalize by 600 so DynamicsCompression can preserve long releases instead of clamping above 100.

Comp._hint: RS Compress amount 0-100 → Threshold full range: 0→+6dB (no comp), 100→-40dB (max).
RS 'Comp' 0-100 → MCompressor Threshold roughly -100 to 0 dB. Higher RS Comp = lower threshold = more compression
RS 'Comp' 0-100 → MCompressor Threshold roughly -100 to 0 dB. Higher RS Comp = lower threshold = more compression

Filter._hint: RS Filter is the multiband crossover in Hz, observed 110-1000. BassMultiComp stores it normalized: (Hz - 110) / 890.

Rate._hint: Higher RS Rate recovers faster.

Compress._hint: RS Compress amount 0-100 → Threshold full range: 0→+6dB (no comp), 100→-40dB (max).
Single-band fallback — RS MBComp's multiband info is lost (Melda free has no MDynamicsMB)

FilterType._hint: RS FilterType moves the Lofinator-style Lo/Hi filter window.

Mix._hint: RS Mix controls drive, resonance, lo-fi texture, and wet intensity.

FilterType._hint: RS FilterType sweeps the low-pass cutoff (low=darker/lo-fi)

Mix._hint: RS Mix → resonance/Q for lo-fi colour (kHs Filter has no dry/wet)

Attack._hint: RS Attack is observed 1-250 ms. BobFilter stores it normalized: (ms - 1) / 249.

Release._hint: RS Release is observed 10-1000 ms. BobFilter stores it normalized: (ms - 10) / 990.

Filter._hint: Rocksmith Filter is a binary 0/1 voicing switch.

FilterType._hint: 0/1/2 maps to Mu-Tron style low-pass / band-pass / high-pass.

Sens._hint: Input detector sensitivity and sweep range. The plugin keeps an internal floor so low RS values still sweep.

Attack._hint: RS Attack is stored as a large raw time value; /1000 keeps it in normalized envelope range.

Release._hint: RS Release is stored as a large raw time value; /1000 keeps it in normalized envelope range.

Sens._hint: floored so low Sens isn't inaudible

FilterType._hint: 0/1/2 maps to AutoSweep low-pass / band-pass / high-pass convention.

Sens._hint: Same AutoSweep sensitivity floor as guitar Auto Tone so low bass values still sweep.

Speed._hint: RS Bass-AutoFilter Speed (if present) → envelope release.

63._hint: RS EQ5 63Hz → KHS Low band. Value is dB direct. Verify paramId in inline editor — may be 'Low Gain', 'Low', or similar. Lossy: bands 250, 2200 are dropped.

100._hint: RS EQ8 100Hz → KHS Low band. dB direct. Lossy: bands 50, 200, 400, 1600, 6400 are dropped — install Voxengo Marvel GEQ for full 8-band fidelity.

75._hint: Bass EQ8 75Hz → KHS Low band. dB direct. Lossy: bands 30, 185, 1100, 6800, 16000 dropped.

Bass._hint: 4-band parametric — value is dB direct. Q + Freq knobs (BassFreq, LoMidFreq, HiMidFreq, TrebleFreq, LoMidQ, HiMidQ) are ignored. Lossy 4→3 (HiMid combined out). For lossless, switch the gear to TDR Nova (free 4-band parametric).

LoMid._hint: Mapped as the single mid; HiMid silently dropped.

Bass._hint: Set Band 1 On = true. dB direct.

LoMidQ._hint: RS LoMidQ ~0-4 — verify Melda Q range matches

HiMidFreq._hint: RS HiMidFreq is in kHz (0.3-8) — multiply by 1000 to land in Hz

TrebleFreq._hint: RS TrebleFreq is in kHz (2-13) — multiply by 1000

Thresh._hint: RS Thresh is stored in dB. Map roughly -100..-50 dB into the bundled gate's normalized threshold.

Rate._hint: Higher RS Rate tightens the gate decay and increases attenuation range.

Thresh._hint: RS Thresh in dB (-66 typical) maps direct to KHS Threshold

Rate._hint: Higher RS Rate = more aggressive gating = larger range

Tone._hint: Scale 0.12 lands roughly at +12 semitones (one octave up) for full Tone=100

Pitch1._hint: RS semitone Pitch1 normalized from -24..+24; -12 maps to 0.25 for a low MF-102 carrier.

Mix._hint: Set Pitch manually to -12 in the VST editor for sub-octave

Depth._hint: RS Depth controls ring-mod wet amount and carrier injection.

Waveform._hint: RS Waveform values are selector-like. 0 is rounded sine/triangle, 1 is sharper square.

Sensitivity._hint: RS Sensitivity controls squelch/envelope sensitivity and carrier range.

Attack._hint: RS Attack controls envelope and carrier slew time.

Freq._hint: RS RingMod Freq → Melda Frequency. Both likely 0-1 normalized; verify range

Limit._hint: Rocksmith sends 0-100-ish limiter amount. The plugin uses an internal threshold curve so low library values still limit audibly.

Rate._hint: Recovery speed; higher Rocksmith Rate recovers faster.

Speed._hint: RS Speed 0-100 → 1-10 Hz (display Hz; range log[0.01,20] in apply_vst_state)

Mix._hint: RS Mix on a tremolo = depth (MTremolo has no Dry/Wet param)
RS Mix controls the chorus/vibe dry-phase blend and modulation depth.

Rate._hint: Alias for any extracted rows that use Rate instead of Speed.

Mix._hint: Rocksmith Multi Vib Mix controls wet/depth intensity.

Waveform._hint: Rocksmith Waveform is selector-like here; value 1 selects the stronger VB-2 LFO/rise character.

Wave._hint: Alias for any extracted rows that use Wave instead of Waveform.

Speed._hint: Alias for any extracted rows that use Speed instead of Rate.

Wave._hint: Rocksmith Wave shapes the Supervibe LFO and sharper rotary/vibe character.

Rate._hint: Target rotary speed. The plugin applies Leslie/LERA-style acceleration and braking internally.

Depth._hint: Doppler, tremolo and cabinet motion intensity.

Balance._hint: Drum/horn balance: lower favors the low drum, higher favors the horn.

Bass._hint: RS Bass pot 0-100 → tone-stack bass pot 0..1 (l). 50 = noon.

Mid._hint: RS Mid pot 0-100 → tone-stack mid pot 0..1 (m). 50 = noon.

Treble._hint: RS Treble pot 0-100 → tone-stack treble pot 0..1 (t). 50 = noon.

BassFreq._hint: RS BassFreq (nominal 1.0) → VST 0.5 = stock bass cap. Scales C2 ±1 octave.

MidShift._hint: RS MidShift (nominal 1.0) → VST 0.5 = stock mid cap. Scales C3 to move the scoop ±1 octave.

TrebleFreq._hint: RS TrebleFreq (nominal 1.0) → VST 0.5 = stock treble cap. Scales C1 ±1 octave.

Bass._hint: RS Bass knob → MEqualizer Band 1. Curator: set Band 1 Frequency ≈ 80 Hz, On = true. dB direct.

Threshold._hint: RS Threshold dB (-27.5..-0.5) → normalized via studiocomp range [-40,0].

Ratio._hint: RS Ratio 1..4 literal → normalized via range [1,12].

Attack._hint: RS Attack 0..92 ms → normalized via range [0,150].

Release._hint: RS Release 50..260 ms → normalized via range [20,500].

Threshold._hint: RS Threshold is dB direct (-27.5..-0.5). Maps 1:1.

Ratio._hint: RS Ratio 1..4 = literal ratio. MCompressor Ratio is the same.

Attack._hint: RS Attack 0..92 ≈ ms direct (Melda Attack is ms). Verify range — may need 0.01 scale if Melda normalizes.

Release._hint: RS Release 50..260 ms direct. MCompressor Release is ms.

Attack._hint: Literal ms; kHs Attack range is log [1,500] ms (see _VST_PARAM_RANGES).

Bass._hint: RS Bass uses roughly -7..+7. Map 0 to the plugin's neutral 0.5.

Mid._hint: RS Mid uses roughly -7..+7. Map 0 to the plugin's neutral 0.5.

Treble._hint: RS Treble uses roughly -7..+7. Map 0 to the plugin's neutral 0.5.

Tone._hint: RS Tone 0-100 → damping (dark↔bright).

Mix._hint: RS Mix 0-100 → wet/dry 0..1.

Tone._hint: RS Tone (brightness) → less Dampen. Inverted.

Rate._hint: RS Rate in Hz (0.2..2.5) → normalized (plugin 0.1..6 Hz).

Mix._hint: RS Mix 0-100 → wet/dry 0..1.

Rate._hint: RS chorus Rate maps to kHs Chorus Rate slider POSITION directly: RS=4 → slider at 4%. Plugin's internal Rate-display conversion (log 0.05..10 Hz) does the rest. User-verified intent.

Delay._hint: Both kHs Chorus and RS use 'Delay' for the base offset

TimeL._hint: RS TimeL in ms (40..360) → 0..700 ms normalized.

TimeR._hint: RS TimeR in ms → 0..700 ms normalized.

TimeL._hint: RS TimeL+TimeR are in ms (0-1000); use TimeL alone, scaled 0-500ms→0-1

Filter._hint: RS Filter on a delay is a feedback tone control

Rate._hint: RS Rate in Hz (0.3..0.6) → normalized (plugin 0.1..6 Hz).

Regen._hint: RS Regen IS feedback in kHs vocabulary

Tone._hint: RS Tone 0-100 → damping (dark↔bright).

Mix._hint: RS Mix 0-100 → wet/dry 0..1.

Tone._hint: RS Tone 0-100 → damping (dark↔bright).

Tone._hint: RS Tone (brightness) → less Dampen. Inverted.
